title: docent film spec
description: The contract between the agent layer (which authors a spec) and the engine (which renders it). Versioned; the engine validates every spec against the structural subset of this in cli/validate.ts.

metrics.description: quantities scenes — figure cards whose number is a tweened value that counts up across beats

nodes.description: structure scenes — the component boxes of the diagram

edges.description: structure scenes — the lines between nodes. `kind` types what the line asserts (relation/feedback/entails/causes)

stages.description: progression scenes — the ordered timeline markers

flow.description: progression scenes — the track topology: a single path (linear), a loop (cycle), two parallel lanes (braided), or a converging loop (iterate)

xAxis.description: chart scenes — the horizontal axis domain

yAxis.description: chart scenes — the vertical axis domain

series.description: chart scenes — the plotted series (line / bars / point)

text.description: passage scenes — the plain-text artifact (a poem, prose, a primary source), typeset as prose/verse with line breaks preserved

marks.description: passage scenes — the annotatable spans of `text`, activated by beats' reveal/focus

image.description: figure scenes — the still image, resolved via staticFile (a bare filename resolves under public/figures/)

callouts.description: figure scenes — labelled markers pinned to normalized regions of `image`, activated by beats' reveal/focus

statement.description: big-idea scenes — the single sentence the viewer should leave with. ≤ 20 words, ends with a period, must not start with 'This is' / 'It is' (a filler opening). Enforced by depthcheck.

anchor.description: big-idea scenes — the visual that lands the statement (a glyph, an equation fragment, an image, or a chart fragment)

systems.description: prior-art scenes — the 2-4 prior systems compared against (columns of the table)

dimensions.description: prior-art scenes — the 2-4 trade-off dimensions compared on (rows of the table)

cells.description: prior-art scenes — one cell per (system, dimension) pair; each marks same/diverges with a short claim

novelty.description: prior-art scenes — which dimension carries the novelty, and the one-liner the film argues from

journeyStages.description: journey-map scenes — a person's emotional arc across 3-8 stages, each with an emotion chip and optional touchpoints / pain points

id.description: unique across the whole film — the TTS cache keys on it

set.description: tween directive — named values this beat drives; the engine eases each from the value the prior set-beat held

transform.description: morph directive — re-binds named nodes to new definitions; the engine eases old→new across this beat (cross-beat object identity)

node.description: a component box in a structure diagram. `as` is its representation (default `box`, the labelled Card); a `transform` can morph one node into another representation.

as.description: the node's representation — `box` is the Card; matrix/vector/grid draw `cells`; `code` a code window; `equation` typesets `expr`

expr.description: as: equation — the mathematical markup the engine typesets. Intent-level and never evaluated; morphing one equation node into another is a derivation step

transform.description: one morph — re-binds a node to a new definition. `node` names an existing node id in the scene; `into` is a partial Node whose fields override the node's prior definition.

node.description: the id of an existing node in this scene

into.description: a partial Node — only the fields it names change

node-partial.description: a partial Node — every field optional; merged onto the node's prior definition

edge.description: a line between two nodes in a structure diagram. `kind` types what the line asserts; `strength` qualifies a causal claim's weight.

from.description: the id of the source node

to.description: the id of the target node

kind.description: what the line asserts — a plain association (relation), a returning loop (feedback), a logical 'therefore' (entails), or a causal claim (causes)

strength.description: qualifies a `causes` edge — a necessary cause is drawn visibly heavier than a contributing one

stage.description: a progression timeline marker. `track` places the stage on one of two braided lanes.

duration.description: shown on the stage's segment, e.g. '4 years'

gate.description: a milestone diamond sitting before this stage

track.description: braided flow — which of the two parallel lanes (0 or 1)

tween.description: a value the engine interpolates across a beat boundary

to.description: the target the beat drives toward

from.description: explicit start value, used only by the first beat to set this key

metric.description: a figure card whose displayed number IS a tweened value

bind.description: the `set` key this metric's number reads from

axis.description: a chart axis — a labelled domain the engine maps onto STAGE pixels

ticks.description: tick marks along the axis; capped at 10 for legibility

series.description: a plotted chart series. `kind` picks the geometry; a `line` names a `fn` from the closed allowlist or gives explicit `points`, `bars` carries `data`, `point` is a marker bound to a curve.

fn.description: line series — a named function from the closed allowlist; never an arbitrary expression

points.description: line series — an explicit polyline, an alternative to `fn`

data.description: bars series — one datum per bar; capped at 8 for legibility

bind.description: point series — a `set` key giving the marker's x

along.description: point series — the line series id whose curve gives the marker's y

mark.description: passage scenes — one annotatable span. `quote` is the exact substring of the scene's `text` to highlight; the engine underlines it and pins `note` beside it. A beat activates marks by id through reveal/focus.

id.description: unique within the scene; beats reveal/focus this id

quote.description: the exact substring of `text` to locate and highlight

note.description: the short annotation pinned to the span

callout.description: figure scenes — one labelled marker pinned to a region of the image. `at` is a normalized 0..1 (x, y) position. A beat activates callouts by id through reveal/focus.

id.description: unique within the scene; beats reveal/focus this id

at.description: normalized [x, y] position over the image, each in 0..1

note.description: an optional longer note under the label

prior-art-system.description: prior-art scenes — one prior system (a column of the comparison table). `year` is optional version/release context (e.g. '2014', 'v3.x'); without it the judge will flag the survey as 'older systems' rather than named ones.

id.description: unique within the scene; beats reveal/focus this id

prior-art-dimension.description: prior-art scenes — one trade-off dimension (a row of the comparison table). The label should name a *choice*, not a quality — 'storage layout' not 'speed'.

id.description: unique within the scene; the novelty dimension references this id

label.description: the dimension's name — a trade-off the field has made differently

prior-art-cell.description: prior-art scenes — one (system, dimension) cell. `mark` is `same` (the system makes the same choice as the subject) or `diverges` (a different choice). `note` is a short dimensional claim, ≤ ~10 words; not 'X is better' but 'X traded A for B'.

system.description: a system id from this scene's `systems`

dimension.description: a dimension id from this scene's `dimensions`

note.description: the dimensional claim — a trade-off, not a verdict

prior-art-novelty.description: prior-art scenes — the named novelty: which of this scene's dimensions the film argues from, and the one-line statement of what is new. The statement must be dimensional ('X is a runtime decision, Y was admission-time'), never evaluative ('X is better than Y').

dimension.description: a dimension id from this scene's `dimensions` — the row the film lights up

statement.description: the one-line novelty: what is new, dimensionally

journey-stage.description: journey-map scenes — one stage on a person's emotional journey. `emotion` is the local feeling (an enum from the JourneyEmotion allowlist); `curveValue` is the y-value on the scene's continuous emotion curve, normalized [0..1] (1 = best emotion, 0 = worst). `touchpoints` are what the person encounters in this stage; `painPoints` are what goes wrong. A journey with no specifics is just a list.

id.description: unique within the scene; beats reveal/focus this id

sub.description: a one-line gloss (e.g. 'a week of trial')

emotion.description: the local feeling — drives the chip's colour and label

touchpoints.description: short bullets — what the person encounters in this stage (docs they read, screens they see, people they talk to)

painPoints.description: short bullets — what goes wrong in this stage (the specific frictions, blockers, confusions)

curveValue.description: the y-value on the emotion curve, normalized 0..1 (1 = top / best emotion, 0 = bottom / worst)
